title: acer rubrum subsp. semiorbiculatum
intro: Acer rubrum subsp. semiorbiculatum, also known as the red maple, is a subspecies of the red maple tree (Acer rubrum) native to eastern North America. It is a deciduous tree with numerous medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Acer rubrum subsp. semiorbiculatum include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the red maple, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
